title: Missing parameter assignment results in Warning: No clocks found in design
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Please see the details of https://github.com/YosysHQ/nextpnr/issues/253 - this is probably more of a feature request than an actual bug.  I've created this issue in yosys per a [comment](https://github.com/YosysHQ/nextpnr/issues/253#issuecomment-475432514) by @koriakin 

> Note that nextpnr doesn't see your original design -- by the time yosys is done with your design, there really is no register left on the clock, and this is what nextpnr sees. It also cannot see that you don't assign to oled, because yosys simplifies that to a constant driver using the initial value (which is a perfectly reasonable design as far as nextpnr is concerned). If you would like a better warning about that, it needs to be in yosys.

## Expected behavior

I'm hoping for a more explicit message than simply `Warning: No clocks found in design` when, as @daveshah1 [noted](https://github.com/YosysHQ/nextpnr/issues/253#issuecomment-475146011):

> This warning is generated when there are no interior paths (ie paths between register output and input) for any clock in the design, as [note here].(https://github.com/YosysHQ/nextpnr/issues/253#issuecomment-475343715) 

## Actual behavior

All (?) issues for "no interior path" apparently result in the same `Warning: No clocks found in design` message. It would be really useful to actually _name the problematic element_.

Admittedly, I'm an FPGA newbie, and this was probably a bonehead mistake by any measure. Still, I spent a tremendous amount of time googling clock issues, when in fact the actual problem was unrelated to the clock. 

If things like this are more easily self resolved with detailed error messages, I think that would help tremendously to expand the use of FPGA in the technical community.  :)
